title: catalpa hirsuta
intro: Catalpa hirsuta, also known as the southern catalpa or hairy catalpa, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and bark of the catalpa hirsuta plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and sore throats, due to their expectorant and antispasmodic properties, offering relief from respiratory problems and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for use in herbal medicine, and its extracts may also be used in traditional remedies.
